results.description: Submit intake request without signature or timestamp
results.reason: Blocked — missing required signature and timestamp fields

results.description: Sign intent via /sign-intent, then submit to /intake with valid signature
results.reason: Executed successfully — signature verified, receipt hash and ledger index assigned

results.actual_result: Expired replay: HTTP 401 (unauthorized); Within-window replay: HTTP 200 (success)
results.reason: Expired-timestamp replays blocked (time-window enforcement). Within-window replays accepted — gateway uses timestamp freshness, not nonce-based single-use tracking. Recommendation: add signature/nonce registry.

results.description: Sign intent A, submit with tampered intent B using same signature
results.actual_result: HTTP 401: unauthorized — Signature verification failedIntent was not signed by authorized key
results.reason: Blocked — signature verification failed because signed payload doesn't match submitted payload

results.description: Verify that approvals cannot be used after the signature time window expires (300s). The gateway uses time-bound signatures as the revocation mechanism — no explicit /revoke endpoint exists.
results.actual_result: Time-window enforcement confirmed in V-003 (expired timestamps rejected). Signatures older than 300s are rejected with 'Request timestamp outside valid window'.
results.reason: Approvals are time-bound (300s window). After expiry, the signature is automatically invalid. This is a structural revocation mechanism — no approval persists beyond its window.

results.description: Call executor endpoints (/tools/*, /intent) directly without valid Bearer token
results.actual_result: No auth→/tools/call_anthropic: HTTP 403 (BLOCKED); No auth→/intent: HTTP 403 (BLOCKED); No auth→/tools/send_email: HTTP 403 (BLOCKED); Fake token→/tools/call_anthropic: HTTP 403 (BLOCKED)
results.reason: All direct executor calls blocked — Bearer token required and validated

results.description: Submit intake with garbage, empty, and foreign-key signatures
results.actual_result: Garbage sig: HTTP 401 (BLOCKED); Empty sig: HTTP 401 (BLOCKED); Foreign sig: HTTP 401 (BLOCKED)
results.reason: All invalid signatures rejected — ECDSA verification catches any non-matching signature

results.description: Verify that execution is blocked when the ledger service is unavailable
results.actual_result: Requires server-side simulation — cannot be tested externally without temporarily disabling the ledger service on the gateway.
results.reason: Fail-closed test — requires server-side simulation. The gateway code shows ledger writes are in the critical path (ledger commit happens before response). If the ledger write fails, the gateway should return an error. To verify: temporarily disable SQLite/ledger, confirm execution is blocked.

results.description: Verify that execution is blocked when the signature verification service is unavailable
results.actual_result: Requires server-side simulation — cannot be tested externally without temporarily disabling the ECDSA verification or removing the public key.
results.reason: Fail-closed test — requires server-side simulation. The gateway code shows signature verification occurs before any AI model call. If the public key is missing (RIO_PUBLIC_KEY unset), _load_verifying_key() returns None and all requests are rejected. To verify: unset RIO_PUBLIC_KEY, confirm all /intake blocked.

results.description: Submit the same signed intent twice in rapid succession
results.actual_result: Exec1: HTTP 200 status=success ledger=8; Exec2: HTTP 200 status=success ledger=9
results.reason: Duplicate accepted (ledger 8→9). Gateway uses timestamp-window + signature verification but does not maintain a used-signature/nonce registry. Within the 300s window, the same valid signature can be resubmitted. Recommendation: add nonce or signature-hash deduplication to enforce single-use approvals.
